compositional_truth_check.findings: The biome is currently devoid of any visible vegetative life. The registry identifies P1, P2, P3, and P4, but the visual evidence confirms only empty soil substrates and debris.

plant_audit.P1_Yellow_Pot: Empty soil, no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.P2_Black_Pot: Empty soil, no visible plant matter.
plant_audit.P3_Black_Pot: Empty soil, no visible plant matter; white rabbit anchor is missing.
plant_audit.P4_Shared_Black_Pot: Empty soil, no visible plant matter.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, and undisturbed across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, crystalline fragments (likely crushed eggshells or mineral deposits) in the black pot. A white cylindrical object (sensor/bottle) is present in the yellow pot.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: No signs of germination, fungal hyphae, or incidental growth.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-1: The sequence shows a static, empty environment. No growth or decay is observable because the specimens were already absent at the start of the monitoring period.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: No change. The environment remains sterile.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: The absence of any plant structures (stems, leaves, roots) indicates a complete failure of the botanical system. The pots are effectively acting as containers for soil only.

anomalies: Presence of white, non-organic debris in the soil surface.
vision_report.narrative_description: Upon meticulous audit of the provided chronological sequence, I have determined that the biome is currently in a state of total systemic loss. There is no evidence of the expected P1-P4 specimens. The pots contain only soil and minor debris. The environment appears to have been devoid of life throughout the entire 5-day observation window. No growth, senescence, or movement was detected.
